question: Why is the buffer-identity decode gain smaller at ctx4096 than at ctx512/1024/2048?
answer_summary: TWO mechanisms, both confirmed; the hypothesis (pure fixed-tax) is REFINED not just confirmed.

(1) FIXED TAX CONFIRMED: the removed materialization E_49152 is ctx-FLAT (~1.52ms, attribution slope +0.003 ms/1k). As a constant absolute saving it shrinks in % terms as the decode denominator grows.

(2) NEW: the replacement whole-cache tile has a STEEPER ctx-slope than the slice tile it replaced (A_tile +0.265 vs B_tile +0.227 ms/1k), so the absolute saving itself ERODES with ctx (saved_ms wall slope -0.090 ms/1k; saved 1.872->1.528ms). This erosion is the larger driver of the shrinking %.
hypothesis_outcome: PARTIALLY CONFIRMED + REFINED: fixed E_49152 tax is real and ctx-flat, but the shrinking % gain is driven MORE by the whole-cache tile's steeper ctx-slope (saved_ms erosion) than by fixed-tax/denominator alone.

actionable_lever.name: whole-cache strided K/V read coalescing in owned_flash_tile_gqa_whole
actionable_lever.rationale: A reads K/V from the strided whole cache buffer (K@0, V@+Hkv*MAXC*Hd); B read a freshly-materialized CONTIGUOUS buffer. The strided read is less ctx-efficient (penalty 0.046ms@512 -> 0.198ms@4096).
why_below_bar: decode already at/above llama (103-106%) across all supported ctx
headroom <2% and concentrated at long ctx only
A stays above llama within MAXC (crossover ~8335 > 4608)
decode primitive space documented as largely exhausted (memory)

decision: NO ACTION NOW (artifact only). Document the bounded whole-cache strided-read lever for a future long-ctx decode push if ever prioritized. No default change, no kernel change, no machine search this task.
do_not: treat PROFILE timing as promotion authority
